Movemental Exemplar · features

  • Home heroes
  • Data viz
  • This page

Approach A · Icon grid

Parallel claims for scanners.

Three or four peer tiles on a section surface; each tile is card with ambient shadow. Icons stay single-weight line art — color comes from primary at low mix, not filled rainbow panels.

Signal, not noise

Rank and surface what actually moved formation — citations, primary sources, and limits stated plainly.

One home

Books, courses, and essays resolve to one domain so readers feel a single editorial mind.

Paced delivery

Ship in slices with review gates — velocity without surrendering voice fidelity or disclosure.

Approach B · Alternating rows

Narrative rhythm for deep work.

Pair copy with an abstract “artifact” panel (tonal blocks, not stock photos). Flip column order on wide screens so the eye zigzags down the page.

Discovery that respects your corpus

Workshops map voice markers, objection patterns, and proof assets before any UI lands in Figma or code. The goal is an IA that can carry a decade of writing without flattening it.

See methodology →

Implementation with editorial QA

Components are reviewed like layout in a magazine: hierarchy, line length, and disclosure placement — not only accessibility and performance (those are table stakes).

How delivery works →

Approach C · Bento grid

Asymmetric emphasis without breaking the ramp.

One midnight cell anchors the grid; supporting cells stay card on section. On small screens the stack order is hero first — progressive enhancement, not a separate mobile story.

Flagship

Movement OS

A coherent stack for leaders who publish across formats: shared taxonomy, shared voice layer, shared evidence footings. Midnight cell signals category definition — use sparingly.

Assessments

Adaptive instruments with leader-owned data models — not black-box scoring for engagement bait.

Learning flows

Cohort pacing, reflection prompts, and exportable artifacts that survive platform churn.

Partner API

Webhooks and structured content for CRMs and grant reporting — documented, versioned, boring on purpose.

More

Static HTML · Movemental tokens
